$schema_comment: Release candidate manifest. Machine-readable declaration of what this release includes, claims, and requires.

required_test_commands.name: Web client tests
required_test_commands.command: cd client && npm ci && npm test

required_test_commands.name: Worker type check
required_test_commands.command: cd deploy/cloudflare-worker && npm ci && npx tsc --noEmit

required_test_commands.name: Plugin tests
required_test_commands.command: cd client && npm ci && cd .. && client/node_modules/.bin/vitest run plugin/tests

required_test_commands.name: Plugin type check
required_test_commands.command: cd deploy/cloudflare-worker && npm ci && cd ../.. && deploy/cloudflare-worker/node_modules/.bin/tsc -p plugin/tsconfig.json --noEmit

security_claims.key_model: Static X25519 identity keypairs (generated once at pairing). Session key uniqueness from fresh random nonces per connection mixed into HKDF salt. The Python SDK supports persisting keypairs across restarts via the KeyPair export. The web reference client keeps its keypair in-memory only — it is stable across reconnections within one page session but lost on page reload (acceptable for a reference client; production clients should persist to IndexedDB).
security_claims.notes: v1 does NOT provide forward secrecy. Compromising an identity private key allows computing the ECDH shared secret and (with captured session nonces) deriving all past and future session keys. ADR needed for v2 (consider Noise IK/XX).

known_limitations: Single relay node only — no clustering, federation, or HA
Stream resumption not supported — interrupted streams require new request
Requests are not idempotent by default — no automatic retries for state-changing methods
TOFU model for gateway identity — no certificate authority or out-of-band verification beyond pairing
JavaScript SDK is not yet implemented
OpenClaw gateway plugin requires a current OpenClaw runtime with channel and CLI plugin APIs enabled
